questions.prompt: What is the visible hard portion of the nail called?
questions.rationale: The nail plate is the visible hard portion of the nail, composed of keratin.

questions.prompt: Which part of the nail is the white half-moon shape at the base?
questions.rationale: The lunula is the white half-moon at the base of the nail.

questions.prompt: What should be done if a client has onychomycosis?
questions.rationale: Onychomycosis is a nail fungus, and barbers should refer clients with this condition to a physician.

questions.prompt: How should metal nail implements be sanitized between clients?
questions.rationale: All metal nail implements must be disinfected in an EPA-registered disinfectant between clients.

questions.prompt: What is the correct action if a client has a bruised nail?
questions.rationale: Bruised nails may be painful, so avoid applying pressure during the service.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a single-use item in nail services?
questions.rationale: Orangewood sticks are single-use items and must be discarded after each client.

questions.prompt: What is the first step in the basic manicure procedure?
questions.rationale: The first step in the basic manicure procedure is to remove existing polish with non-acetone remover.

questions.prompt: What shape can nails be filed into during a manicure?
questions.rationale: Nails can be filed into square, oval, or round shapes during a manicure.

questions.prompt: What should be done if a client has an ingrown nail that is infected?
questions.rationale: Ingrown nails that are infected require medical attention, so refer the client to a physician.

questions.prompt: What is the purpose of applying cuticle oil during a manicure?
questions.rationale: Cuticle oil is applied to moisturize and nourish the cuticle area.

questions.prompt: During a manicure, why should living tissue not be cut?
questions.rationale: Cutting living tissue can lead to infection and should be avoided.

questions.prompt: What should be done with foot baths after each client?
questions.rationale: Foot baths must be cleaned and disinfected per Indiana state board requirements after each client.

questions.prompt: Which nail disorder presents as thickened, discolored, brittle nails?
questions.rationale: Onychomycosis, or nail fungus, presents as thickened, discolored, brittle nails.

questions.prompt: What is the final step in the basic manicure procedure?
questions.rationale: The final step in the basic manicure procedure is to clean up edges with a brush dipped in remover.

questions.prompt: Why should non-acetone remover be used to remove existing polish?
questions.rationale: Non-acetone remover is less drying to the nails and surrounding skin.

questions.prompt: What should be done if a client has hangnails?
questions.rationale: Moisturizing the area can help prevent hangnails from worsening.

questions.prompt: What is the appropriate action if a nail implement falls on the floor?
questions.rationale: Any implement that falls on the floor must be disinfected in an EPA-registered disinfectant before use.

questions.prompt: How long should hands be soaked in warm soapy water during a manicure?
questions.rationale: Hands should be soaked in warm soapy water for 3-5 minutes to soften the skin and nails.

questions.prompt: What is the purpose of buffing the nail surface during a manicure?
questions.rationale: Buffing the nail surface helps to remove ridges and create a smooth surface for polish application.

questions.prompt: What is an important sanitation requirement for single-use items?
choices: They must be discarded after each client
questions.rationale: Single-use items must be discarded after each client to prevent cross-contamination.

flashcards.definition: The visible hard portion of the nail, composed of keratin.
The skin beneath the nail plate.
The white half-moon shape at the base of the nail.
The overlapping skin at the base of the nail.
The part of the nail that extends beyond the fingertip.
A nail fungus presenting as thickened, discolored, brittle nails.
A torn piece of skin at the edge of the nail.
Also known as subungual hematoma, appears dark and may be painful.
A nail that grows into the skin, requiring medical attention if infected.
A disinfectant approved by the Environmental Protection Agency for use on metal implements.
Items like nail files and buffers that must be discarded after each client.
Remove existing polish with non-acetone remover.
Push back cuticles gently with an orangewood stick.
The barber exam includes questions on nail anatomy, disorders, and sanitation.
Foot baths must be cleaned and disinfected per Indiana state board requirements.
